description: ByteDance LatentSync — audio-driven LIP-SYNC. Give it a talking-head video and an audio track; LatentSync re-renders the mouth so it matches the speech (latent-diffusion lip sync, Whisper audio features + SyncNet supervision). A SEPARATE ecosystem from LTX (its own ComfyUI wrapper, weights and base). Graph: ShmuelRonen/ComfyUI-LatentSyncWrapper 'latentsync1.5_comfyui_basic'. NOTE: the wrapper pip-installs native deps (mediapipe / decord / face-alignment) on first load and manages an auxiliary model cache — see _RISK; --convert-only is the cheap test that those deps install on the image.

about.what: Re-syncs a talking person's mouth to a separate audio track — true dubbing: keep the video, swap the speech, and the lips follow the new audio.
about.inputs: A talking-head video (25fps, frontal face works best) and an audio file with the speech to sync to.
about.key_knobs: Video + audio (both required). Lip expressiveness and inference steps are advanced knobs on the node.

_RISK: UNVERIFIED on Spark. Two risks the gate/render must clear: (1) NATIVE DEPS — the wrapper's requirements (mediapipe, decord, face-alignment, DeepCache) must pip-install on the yanwk cu130 image; if a wheel fails, this needs a custom image with them baked. --convert-only tests this (it clones + installs the pack). (2) RUNTIME AUTODOWNLOAD — the wrapper fetches auxiliary models (s3fd, etc) to ~/.latentsync16_models at node-execute time; the Spark node has network, so this should work at render. The private LatentSync weights are provided here from the chunyu-li mirror, placed in the wrapper's own checkpoints/ dir.
_WEIGHTS: Placed in the wrapper's OWN dir (custom_nodes/ComfyUI-LatentSyncWrapper/checkpoints/), NOT models/, because the node hardcodes that path. `stable_syncnet.pt` is the chunyu-li `latentsync_syncnet.pt` saved under the name the wrapper expects. The SD VAE is the standard sd-vae-ft-mse. If the wrapper wants a different layout/version (1.6 vs 1.5), adjust dests after the first render.
_INPUTS: TWO inputs: the video (positional / --input) and the audio (--face, reused as the generic secondary-input slot — it just stages the file and patches LoadAudio node 37). e.g. `--preset latentsync clip.mp4 --face speech.wav`.

ui.title: LatentSync — audio-driven lip-sync (dub a talking head to new speech)

primary_input.label: Talking-head video (25fps, frontal)
primary_input.tooltip: The clip whose mouth gets re-synced. 25fps and a clear frontal face work best.

secondary_input.label: Audio track (the speech to sync to)
secondary_input.tooltip: The audio whose speech the mouth should follow. (Mapped through the generic secondary-input slot.)

ui.label: Seed
ui.tooltip: Random seed for the lip-sync diffusion. Change to reroll, fix to reproduce.

ui.label: Inference steps
ui.tooltip: Lip-sync diffusion steps (20 default). The graph predates this input so the preset supplies it; the installed LatentSyncNode requires it.

ui.label: Lip expressiveness
ui.tooltip: How pronounced the mouth movement is (1.5 default). Higher = more expressive lips. Also supplied by the preset (graph predates this required input).
